tweet: Bert Snelling’s neighbor told a Virginian-Pilot reporter: “It’s the world we live in now, and it’s a shame.” A friend of Missy Langer’s told The Washington Post: “This kind of stuff has got to stop.” Another friend said: “She didn’t deserve to die like that.”  None of them did.

name: Rep. Elaine Luria
tweet: Those who knew the victims are making statements that we need to hear.

name: Rep. Elaine Luria

name: Rep. Elaine Luria
tweet: I saw love and hope at heartfelt vigils across our community where people of all faiths joined in prayer to remember those who left us all too soon.

name: Rep. Elaine Luria
tweet: I saw unbelievable bravery at Sentara Hospital, where Sunday I had the honor of visiting a survivor of the shooting. She clasped my hand as I stood at her bedside, and her strength and resolve were palpable, as she had only narrowly come away alive.

name: Rep. Elaine Luria
tweet: That includes including the officer, among the first responders, who in the performance of his duties took a bullet to protect our community, and was by the grace of God saved by his protective vest.

tweet: Today I voted to protect Virginia’s historic Medicaid expansion & overall access to affordable health care.  Our focus should be lowering health care costs & the price of prescription drugs – not gutting protections for people with pre-existing conditions.  https://t.co/PNxWPMecvp

name: Rep. Elaine Luria
tweet: Shutdown, Day 19.  Families are hurting.  Nearly 30K Virginians — Coast Guard members, TSA staff, and more — are furloughed or working without pay.  That’s why I’ve directed the Chief Administrative Officer of the House to withhold my pay until federal employees are made whole.  https://t.co/bKduAOgKjQ

name: Rep. Elaine Luria
tweet: Tonight the President said it’s “immoral” to “do nothing,” but the House has passed bipartisan legislation that would open government.  It’s long past time for this 18-day shutdown to end.  Our Coast Guard, TSA, and other federal workers shouldn’t be hostages in political fights.
